MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's Motion To Reconsider Mtn To Vacate Summary Judgment (New Evidence).

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. The motion does not comply with CCP section 1008. The statute requires the motion to be served and filed within 10 days of the original order. That order was served September 5, and plaintiff filed the present motion September 21. This is 16 days. Even accounting for additional time for service, the motion is late. Section 1008 requires new or different facts. Even after a careful review of plaintiff's moving papers, and his "Answer to Regents Opposition," there are obviously no new facts.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/CEK)

Off Calendar May-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES
Tibor Szabo (415.424.2981; artisteseventssf@gmail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Rhonda Woo, Esq. (650.365.7715; rwoo@hrmrlaw.com) of Ridley Master for Moving party VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE.

The court notes there is no compliance with tentative ruling rule. Matter is discussed.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant's (THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA) demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the causes of action for breach of contract, defamation, slander, fraud, violation of privacy, assault, conversion, violation of the computer abuse act, invasion of privacy, trespass, and breach of fiduciary duty.

The cause of action for breach of contract fails to state a cause of action and whether the purported contract is written, oral or implied. (CCP 430.10(e) & (g).) Plaintiff does not clearly allege how he entered into an agreement with defendant, his performance or excuse, or defendant's breach. The original complaint, which the court takes judicial notice of, indicates that plaintiff's agreement was solely with Mr. Protacio. (Original Complaint, 2:7 ["Matt has regular job at UCSF and this was a second business."].) Plaintiff has leave to amend to cure these defects.

The above-cited tort causes of action fail under Gov't Code section 815.2. That provision provides that defendant, a public entity, can be liable for Mr. Protacio's conduct if it were done "within the scope of his employment." Plaintiff pleads no facts to indicate that the alleged torts were within the scope of Mr. Protacio's employment with defendant. (CCP 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing that the torts were in the course and scope of Mr. Protacio's employment, a conspiracy existed between defendant and Mr. Protacio, that defendant was an aider and abettor, or another basis for liability.

The demurrers to the perjury and spoliation causes of action are sustained without leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).) Those claims are not cognizable as a matter of law. (See Pollock v. University of Southern California (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 1416, 1429 [no civil cause of action for perjury]; Temple Community Hospital (1999) 20 Cal.4th 464, 466 [no cause of action for intentional destruction or suppression of evidence].)

The remaining demurrers are overruled. Defendant fails to show that the co-tortfeasors are necessary parties. Defendant also fails to show that abatement is proper since the same parties and same causes of action are not implicated in those suits. The court notes that defendant is not named in those actions.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
